**Clinical Reasoning:** None of the retrieved articles mention the term “July effect” or reference any seasonal variation in surgical outcomes, particularly in the context of major cancer surgeries. All content focuses on treatment indications, patient-centered decision-making, surgical techniques, and outcomes related to specific cancer types—none addressing temporal patterns like seasonality. Thus, there is no support for the existence of a July effect in these settings.
